title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager CVE-2026-20122 and CVE-2026-20128 Under Active Exploitation: Authenticated File Overwrite and DCA Credential Disclosure Enable Web Shell Deployment and Cross-Instance Lateral Movement in Enterprise SD-WAN Infrastructure
affected_products: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (vManage) prior to 20.9.8.2
Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager 20.12.x prior to 20.12.6.1
Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager 20.12.x prior to 20.12.5.3
Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager 20.15.x prior to 20.15.4.2
Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager prior to 20.18.2.1

CISA Emergency Directive ED 26-03 - Mitigate Vulnerabilities in Cisco SD-WAN Systems

timeline.event: Threat actor UAT-8616 (suspected nation-state) begins exploiting Cisco SD-WAN infrastructure vulnerabilities for persistent access to enterprise networks
Cisco publishes security advisory cisco-sa-sdwan-authbp-qwCX8D4v disclosing multiple SD-WAN vulnerabilities including CVE-2026-20122 and CVE-2026-20128
CISA issues Emergency Directive ED-26-03 requiring federal agencies to address Cisco SD-WAN vulnerabilities; joint advisory published with NSA, NCSC-UK, NCSC-NZ, ACSC, Cyber Centre
Massive exploitation spike observed — threat actors launch large-scale attacks against SD-WAN Manager instances; web shell deployment confirmed by security researchers
Cisco PSIRT formally updates advisory to confirm active in-the-wild exploitation of CVE-2026-20122 and CVE-2026-20128; urgent patching mandated
CISA adds CVE-2026-20122 and CVE-2026-20128 to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog; FCEB agencies required to patch by March 26, 2026
Active exploitation continues; organizations conducting incident response and threat hunting for UAT-8616 indicators of compromise

simple_summary.what_happened: Cisco has confirmed that attackers are actively exploiting two vulnerabilities in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (also known as vManage), the central management software that enterprises use to control their entire software-defined wide-area networks. CVE-2026-20122 allows an attacker with low-level API credentials to overwrite arbitrary files on the SD-WAN Manager server — a capability that has been used to deploy persistent web shells. CVE-2026-20128 allows an attacker with minimal access to read a credential file for the Data Collection Agent (DCA), enabling them to authenticate to other SD-WAN Manager instances and move laterally across an organization's entire SD-WAN deployment. These two vulnerabilities are typically chained together or combined with the previously-disclosed CVE-2026-20127 authentication bypass. A massive exploitation spike was observed on March 4, 2026, with web shell deployments confirmed.
simple_summary.who_is_affected: Organizations running Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (vManage) versions prior to 20.9.8.2, 20.12.6.1, 20.12.5.3, 20.15.4.2, or 20.18.2.1. This affects enterprises, government agencies, telecommunications companies, and service providers who use Cisco SD-WAN for managing distributed network infrastructure across branch offices, data centers, and cloud environments. FCEB agencies with unpatched systems face a CISA-mandated March 26, 2026 deadline.
simple_summary.what_attackers_do: Step 1: Attackers gain initial access to the Cisco SD-WAN Manager either by exploiting the related CVE-2026-20127 authentication bypass (which requires no credentials at all) or by obtaining low-privilege API credentials through phishing or credential stuffing attacks. Step 2: Using their initial foothold, attackers exploit CVE-2026-20128 to read the Data Collection Agent (DCA) credential file from the filesystem — this file stores DCA user passwords with insufficient access controls, allowing any low-privilege user to read it. Step 3: Attackers use the harvested DCA credentials to authenticate to additional Cisco SD-WAN Manager instances within the organization's environment, enabling lateral movement across multiple SD-WAN deployments. Step 4: Attackers exploit CVE-2026-20122 to abuse a file overwrite vulnerability in the vManage API, uploading and writing a web shell to the SD-WAN Manager web root directory, establishing a persistent backdoor that survives patches and reboots. Step 5: Through the web shell and administrative access to vManage, attackers gain centralized control over potentially thousands of SD-WAN edge devices (vEdge routers), allowing them to intercept branch office traffic, exfiltrate network configurations and encryption keys, modify routing policies, and pivot to any site connected to the SD-WAN fabric.
what_you_should_do: Immediately patch Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager to versions 20.9.8.2, 20.12.6.1, 20.12.5.3, 20.15.4.2, or 20.18.2.1 or later — FCEB agencies must complete this by March 26, 2026
Hunt for web shells in the vManage web root directories and check for unauthorized files created after February 25, 2026
Audit all user accounts in vManage for unauthorized additions, and check /root/.ssh/authorized_keys for unrecognized SSH keys
Review bash_history and cli-history files — cleared or truncated history files are a strong indicator of post-compromise cleanup by UAT-8616
Restrict vManage API access to trusted IP ranges only and enforce multi-factor authentication for all administrator accounts
simple_summary.why_this_is_serious: Cisco SD-WAN Manager is the nerve center of enterprise SD-WAN deployments — compromising it gives attackers centralized control over all connected branch offices, data centers, and cloud sites simultaneously. Unlike a compromise of a single endpoint, a vManage compromise can affect thousands of edge devices at once. The threat actor UAT-8616 has been operating since at least 2023 and uses sophisticated evasion techniques including log deletion and temporary account creation/removal to avoid detection. CISA, NSA, and Five Eyes intelligence agencies have all issued coordinated advisories on this threat, underscoring its severity to critical infrastructure globally.

tactics.description: CVE-2026-20122 exploited via authenticated API access to the Cisco vManage web interface, typically following initial access via CVE-2026-20127 authentication bypass
CVE-2026-20128 allows reading of the DCA credential file from the filesystem with improper permissions, extracting plaintext or weakly-protected DCA user passwords
CVE-2026-20122 file overwrite vulnerability exploited to deploy web shells in vManage web root directories, providing persistent command execution capability
UAT-8616 adds unauthorized SSH public keys to /root/.ssh/authorized_keys for persistent root-level SSH access
DCA credentials harvested via CVE-2026-20128 used to authenticate to additional SD-WAN Manager instances, enabling lateral movement across the entire SD-WAN management plane

tactics.technique_name: Indicator Removal: Clear Unix/Linux System Logs
tactics.description: UAT-8616 clears bash_history, cli-history, and system log files after compromise to remove forensic evidence of intrusion activity
Centralized vManage access enables automated collection and exfiltration of network configurations, encryption keys, routing policies, and management credentials across all connected SD-WAN sites

detection_guidance.summary: Detection requires hunting for web shell artifacts, unauthorized account modifications, and log tampering on Cisco vManage instances. Review authentication logs from March 4-5, 2026 for exploitation spikes. The presence of cleared bash_history files and unexpected SSH authorized keys are high-fidelity indicators of UAT-8616 post-exploitation activity.

log_sources.description: Authentication events for vManage access, including API login attempts, SSH connections, and user account activities
HTTP access logs for the vManage web interface, especially POST requests to file upload API endpoints and access to newly-created .jsp/.py files in web directories
Command history files — their absence (zero bytes, deletion) is a strong indicator of UAT-8616 post-compromise cleanup
File creation timestamps and contents in vManage web directories, especially new files created after February 25, 2026 that were not deployed by legitimate administrators
Data Collection Agent logs showing authentication events, especially access from unexpected source systems that may indicate credential harvesting exploitation

what_to_look_for.indicator: Cleared or zero-byte bash_history / cli-history files
what_to_look_for.description: UAT-8616 routinely deletes bash_history and cli-history files after intrusion to eliminate evidence of their commands. A zero-byte or missing history file on vManage is a high-confidence indicator of compromise.

what_to_look_for.indicator: Unexpected files in vManage web root directories
what_to_look_for.description: Web shells deployed via CVE-2026-20122 appear as new .jsp, .war, .py, or .sh files in vManage web directories. Any file created after February 25, 2026 that is not part of a legitimate patch deployment should be investigated.
UAT-8616 adds attacker-controlled SSH public keys to /root/.ssh/authorized_keys. Any unrecognized public keys in this file indicate persistent root-level backdoor access.
what_to_look_for.log_field: file='/root/.ssh/authorized_keys' AND key NOT IN authorized_admin_keys

what_to_look_for.indicator: vManage API file upload requests from unexpected IPs
what_to_look_for.description: POST requests to vManage API file management endpoints from IP addresses not in the authorized administrator range, especially during the March 4-5, 2026 exploitation window
what_to_look_for.log_field: api_endpoint CONTAINS '/dataservice/disasterrecovery' AND src_ip NOT IN trusted_admin_range

what_to_look_for.description: File access events for DCA password/credential files in the vManage filesystem from processes or users that should not normally require DCA credentials
what_to_look_for.log_field: file_access_event AND file_path CONTAINS 'dca' AND file_extension IN ('.json', '.conf', '.properties')

immediate_actions.action: Apply Cisco SD-WAN Manager patches immediately
immediate_actions.description: Upgrade Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (vManage) to the patched versions: 20.9.8.2+, 20.12.6.1+, 20.12.5.3+, 20.15.4.2+, or 20.18.2.1+. FCEB agencies must complete patching by March 26, 2026. Consult Cisco advisory cisco-sa-sdwan-authbp-qwCX8D4v for specific version guidance.

immediate_actions.action: Conduct immediate threat hunt on vManage instances
immediate_actions.description: Search for web shells in vManage web root directories, check bash_history/cli-history files for deletion evidence, audit /root/.ssh/authorized_keys for unauthorized keys, and review user accounts for any additions made after February 25, 2026.

immediate_actions.action: Restrict vManage API and management interface access
immediate_actions.description: Immediately implement network ACLs to restrict access to the vManage management interface (HTTPS port 8443/443 and API port 8443) to only authorized administrator IP ranges. Remove any unnecessary external network access to vManage.

immediate_actions.action: Audit and rotate all vManage credentials
immediate_actions.description: Review all administrative accounts in vManage, remove any unrecognized accounts, and rotate passwords for all API users, especially the Data Collection Agent (DCA) credentials. Monitor for unauthorized logins using old credentials post-rotation.

immediate_actions.action: Review the Cisco/CISA SD-WAN Threat Hunt Guide
immediate_actions.description: Download and implement the co-sealed threat hunt guide published by CISA, ASD ACSC, and Five Eyes partners specifically for detecting UAT-8616 indicators of compromise in Cisco SD-WAN environments. Report confirmed compromises to CISA.
